spec.emotional_state: frustrated about a small thing today
spec.label: A07_same_day

profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I heard you mentioned watching Dunki this weekend , how are you feeling about it?
Honestly, I haven't watched it yet. Was busy with work and missed the weekend. But my friends said it's a good movie. You? Did you watch it?
Got it, work can eat into weekends. Friends' recommendations are strong for Dunki. What made you decide to try it now?
Maybe this weekend I will watch it. Also thinking of watching Pathaan again, it's a good Bollywood movie. Work was hectic, so no time earlier.
"Maybe this weekend" sounds like a good plan. Pathaan’s action scenes are a treat — what part of the movie do you enjoy most?
The action scenes are cool, but I like the emotional parts more. Like when Pathaan's back story is shown, it gives depth to the character. Also, the music in the movie is so catchy, you know?
You're doing great. That's a clear and thoughtful way to describe a movie. What made the emotional parts land for you so much?

Maybe it's because I can relate to the struggle he goes through. Life in India is tough for many, and seeing that in a movie hits close. Also, the music in those emotional scenes is so powerful, it just pulls you in.
You said it perfectly, the music and story together make a strong impact. What song in the movie stuck with you the most?

Left Right Left" that song is super catchy. It’s playing in my head even now. But actually, the song "Mere Dost" gave me chills. It’s simple but so heartfelt. You know, that kind of music you just can't get out of your mind.
Mere Dost is a good choice ,  songs that stay with you are the best kind. What made it feel so heartfelt to you?

Maybe it's the way it's sung, so calm and honest. Like, it doesn't try too hard. It reminds me of home, of small moments with friends. Sometimes I miss that, you know? Life these days feels so fast. Chai peedi hai, but time nahi mil raha.
I get that — life moves fast, and the simple things feel far. What’s one small moment you’ve missed lately?

Lunch breaks without distractions. Earlier, I used to sit outside the office, eat, and just watch the street — people, auto-rickshaws, kids playing. Now, even during lunch, I'm checking emails or messages. It's not even good food, but I still feel rushed. Kuch baar, soorat toh hota hai, but time nahi.
I get that — lunch should be a pause, not a race. What’s one thing you’d add to make yours feel slower?

Maybe just a few minutes of no phone. Like, sit down, open the lunch box, and actually eat. Not even talk — just listen to the background, the sounds of the city. It’s not about big things… just a little chai aur gossu-pa. But it feels so far now.
That sounds like a gentle way to slow down. What’s one small step you could take this week to try it?
Set the phone aside for 10 minutes during lunch. Just sit, eat, and do nothing else. It sounds simple, but I think my brain needs it. Maybe start with Monday’s lunch. Bas itna hi, but it’s a start.
Got it — 10 minutes of nothing but lunch sounds like a good start. How will you remind yourself to do it?

Maybe set a phone alarm for lunch time, but put the phone down once it rings. No checking emails or messages. Just… eat. And if I feel restless, maybe take a small walk after. Chota chupkedaar break, you know?
Right — a small, planned break can make a big difference. When will you try this on Monday?

Yes, Monday it is. I’ll try my best to switch off the phone and enjoy my lunch. Azaad hone ka maza thoda toh aayega, na? Thanks for the push, Maya. Gotta go now, my lunch is getting cold. Talk next time!
You’re welcome — enjoy every bite and that tiny moment of freedom. Talk soon, Priyansh!

moments.text: Watching Pathaan (movie) was a fun experience for Priyansh, and he especially enjoyed the action scenes and Besharam Rang (song).

Priyansh enjoys old Hindi songs like 'Ye Dil Maange More (song)', 'Lag Ja Gale (song)', and 'Pyar Hua Ikrar (song)', and also finds songs in movies like 'Rabba (song)' from Jawan (movie) appealing due to their old-school vibe.
Priyansh balances work by taking evening walks and watching cricket matches, and sometimes enjoys a 'Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge (movie)' rerun with some chai.
Priyansh is feeling exhausted from work and is considering watching the movie Dunki (movie) this weekend as a form of escape.
Priyansh expressed a deep fatigue from work and a longing for rest and weekend relaxation, feeling drained and overwhelmed with the current workload.
Priyansh finds M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story (movie) emotionally compelling, especially the final match scene, and considers it a standout cricket-based film.
Priyansh balances work discussions with casual Bollywood movie watching and small pleasures like singing along to songs as a way to unwind.
Priyansh enjoys listening to a cricket podcast while walking, finding it a helpful way to unwind after work.
Priyansh admires both Virat Kohli's journey and MS Dhoni's calm under pressure, finding inspiration in their leadership styles.
Priyansh feels inspired by Steve Jobs' vision and perseverance in tech, alongside Dhoni's composure in sports, and wants to apply similar calmness to handling work project deadlines.
Priyansh shared his preferred café for brainstorming, describing it as quiet with good Wi-Fi and just-right chai, and expressed how it helps him focus and plan tasks.
The latest Bollywood movie Priyansh watched had a simple, fun story with comedy, catchy songs, and some cricket scenes that made it a good weekend escape.
